headline: Born Here, Documented Nowhere
context: An estimated 50,000 to 100,000 children born in Malaysia lack citizenship due to gaps in the Federal Constitution, statelessness of parents, or administrative failures.

cards.big: Born in Malaysian hospitals. Cannot attend school. Cannot work.
cards.sub: Stateless children have no IC, no access to public education, and no legal path forward.

cards.big: Article 14 grants citizenship by birth — but not to all births.
cards.sub: Children of Malaysian mothers married to foreign fathers were excluded until the 2024 amendment stalled.

cards.big: NRD rejection rates for citizenship applications: estimated over 90%.
cards.sub: Appeals take 5 to 15 years. Children age out of school while waiting.

cards.big: Sabah alone accounts for an estimated 40,000 stateless children.
cards.sub: Legacy of the 1990s IC project and undocumented migration creates generational exclusion.

cards.big: A child did not choose where to be born.
cards.sub: Punishing children for their parents' status is not immigration policy. It is cruelty with paperwork.

cards.big: No child should be stateless in the country of their birth. The Constitution can fix this. The question is whether Parliament will.
